1→# Getting Started
     2→
     3→This guide walks you through setting up AgentRail on a project for the
     4→first time, from installation through your first agent-driven session.
     5→
     6→## Prerequisites
     7→
     8→- Rust toolchain (for building agentrail)
     9→- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) (or another AI coding agent)
    10→- [sw-install](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/sw-install) (optional,
    11→  for installing to PATH)
    12→
    13→## Step 1: Install AgentRail
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→# Clone the repo
    17→git clone https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-rs
    18→cd agentrail-rs
    19→
    20→# Build release binary
    21→cargo build --release
    22→
    23→# Install to PATH (via sw-install)
    24→sw-install -p .
    25→
    26→# Verify
    27→agentrail --version
    28→```
    29→
    30→## Step 2: Set Up Your Project
    31→
    32→### Option A: Interactive Wizard (recommended)
    33→
    34→The wizard walks you through project setup with prompts:
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→cd ~/my-project
    38→bash /path/to/agentrail-rs/scripts/agentrail-wizard.sh
    39→```
    40→
    41→It asks for:
    42→1. Project name
    43→2. Plan (what needs to be done, multi-line)
    44→3. Domain repo path (optional, for skill injection)
    45→4. First step (slug, description, role, task type)
    46→5. Planned future steps
    47→
    48→Then it runs `agentrail setup` and creates the first step automatically.
    49→
    50→### Option B: One Command
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→cd ~/my-project
    54→agentrail setup --name my-project --plan "Build a web app with user auth"
    55→```
    56→
    57→This creates:
    58→- `.agentrail/` directory with saga, steps, trajectories, sessions
    59→- `CLAUDE.md` with the AgentRail session protocol (tells Claude how
    60→  to use agentrail commands)
    61→
    62→To also register a domain repo for skill injection:
    63→
    64→```bash
    65→agentrail setup --name my-project \
    66→  --plan "Build a web app" \
    67→  --domain /path/to/agentrail-domain-coding
    68→```
    69→
    70→### Option C: Step by Step
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→cd ~/my-project
    74→
    75→# Initialize the saga
    76→agentrail init --name my-project --plan "Build a web app with user auth"
    77→
    78→# Create CLAUDE.md (copy the template from below)
    79→# Register a domain repo (optional)
    80→```
    81→
    82→## Step 3: Create the First Step
    83→
    84→After setup, create the first step that tells the agent what to do:
    85→
    86→```bash
    87→agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \
    88→  --next-slug scaffold \
    89→  --next-prompt "Set up the project structure: create directories, config files, and a hello-world main" \
    90→  --next-role production \
    91→  --next-task-type rust-project-init
    92→```
    93→
    94→To plan ahead (recommended):
    95→
    96→```bash
    97→agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \
    98→  --next-slug scaffold \
    99→  --next-prompt "Set up the project structure" \
   100→  --next-role production \
   101→  --planned "implement: Build the core logic" \
   102→  --planned "test: Write tests" \
   103→  --planned "docs: Write documentation"
   104→```
   105→
   106→Verify with:
   107→
   108→```bash
   109→agentrail next
   110→```
   111→
   112→You should see the plan, step list, current step prompt, and any
   113→matching skill docs.
   114→
   115→## Step 4: Start Claude Code
   116→
   117→```bash
   118→cd ~/my-project
   119→claude "go"
   120→```
   121→
   122→The agent reads CLAUDE.md, follows the 6-step protocol:
   123→1. `agentrail next` -- sees the plan, step, prompt, skills
   124→2. `agentrail begin` -- marks step as in-progress
   125→3. Does the work described in the prompt
   126→4. Commits code with git
   127→5. `agentrail complete` -- records trajectory, advances to next step
   128→6. Stops
   129→
   130→When the session ends, start a new one for the next step:
   131→
   132→```bash
   133→claude "go"
   134→```
   135→
   136→## Step 5: Repeat
   137→
   138→Each `claude "go"` session handles one step. The agent picks up where
   139→the previous one left off, seeing the full history and any accumulated
   140→skill knowledge.
   141→
   142→After several steps, you can distill trajectories into skill docs:
   143→
   144→```bash
   145→agentrail distill rust-project-init
   146→```
   147→
   148→## CLAUDE.md Template
   149→
   150→If you used `agentrail setup`, this was created automatically. If not,
   151→create `CLAUDE.md` in your project root with this content:
   152→
   153→```markdown
   154→# CLAUDE.md
   155→
   156→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when
   157→working with code in this repository.
   158→
   159→## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)
   160→
   161→This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:
   162→
   163→### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
   164→agentrail next
   165→
   166→### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
   167→agentrail begin
   168→
   169→### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
   170→Do NOT ask "want me to proceed?". The step prompt IS your instruction.
   171→
   172→### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
   173→Commit your code changes with git.
   174→
   175→### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing)
   176→agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
   177→  --reward 1 --actions "tools and approach used"
   178→
   179→### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working)
   180→Do NOT make further code changes after complete.
   181→Any changes after complete are untracked.
   182→```
   183→
   184→Add project-specific sections below the protocol (build commands,
   185→related projects, available task types, etc.).
   186→
   187→## Using a Domain Repo
   188→
   189→Domain repos provide reusable skills across projects. The
   190→[agentrail-domain-coding](https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding)
   191→repo has skills for Rust, C, Lisp, and web development.
   192→
   193→### Register in your project
   194→
   195→```bash
   196→# Clone the domain repo
   197→git clone https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding
   198→
   199→# Register it (creates .agentrail/domains.toml)
   200→agentrail setup --name my-project --plan "..." \
   201→  --domain /path/to/agentrail-domain-coding
   202→```
   203→
   204→Or manually create `.agentrail/domains.toml`:
   205→
   206→```toml
   207→[[domain]]
   208→name = "coding"
   209→path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-coding"
   210→```
   211→
   212→### How it works
   213→
   214→When a step has a `task_type` (e.g., `rust-project-init`), `agentrail
   215→next` looks for a matching skill doc:
   216→1. First in `.agentrail/skills/` (local, project-specific)
   217→2. Then in registered domain repos
   218→
   219→The skill procedure, success patterns, and failure modes are injected
   220→into the agent's prompt automatically.
   221→
   222→## Working on Existing Projects
   223→
   224→For an existing project that doesn't have a saga yet:
   225→
   226→```bash
   227→cd ~/existing-project
   228→
   229→# The wizard works on existing projects too
   230→bash /path/to/agentrail-rs/scripts/agentrail-wizard.sh
   231→
   232→# Or one command
   233→agentrail setup --name existing-project \
   234→  --plan "Add feature X to the existing codebase"
   235→```
   236→
   237→AgentRail adds `.agentrail/` and `CLAUDE.md` without touching your
   238→existing code.
   239→
   240→## After a Saga Completes
   241→
   242→When all steps are done:
   243→
   244→- **More multi-step work**: start a new saga with `agentrail init`
   245→- **Quick one-off fix**: just use `claude` normally without agentrail
   246→- **Add more steps**: `agentrail add --slug fix-bug --prompt "Fix X"`
   247→  (auto-reopens the saga)
   248→
   249→## Maintenance Mode
   250→
   251→For ongoing work without a fixed endpoint (bug fixes, features,
   252→improvements), use maintenance mode:
   253→
   254→```bash
   255→# Just tell Claude what to do -- no pre-planning needed
   256→claude "fix the login timeout bug in auth.rs"
   257→claude "add dark mode to the settings page"
   258→claude "update all dependencies"
   259→```
   260→
   261→The agent creates the step itself via `agentrail add`, does the work,
   262→completes, and stops. Or pre-load a batch:
   263→
   264→```bash
   265→agentrail add --slug fix-123 --prompt "Fix login timeout"
   266→agentrail add --slug fix-124 --prompt "Fix mobile layout"
   267→claude "go"   # does fix-123
   268→claude "go"   # does fix-124
   269→```
   270→
   271→See `docs/maintenance-mode.md` for the full guide and CLAUDE.md template.
   272→
   273→See `docs/user-guide.md` for details.
   274→
   275→## Example: Real Project Setup
   276→
   277→Here is how the [tml24c](https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/tml24c)
   278→project (Tiny Macro Lisp for COR24) was bootstrapped:
   279→
   280→```bash
   281→cd ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tml24c
   282→
   283→# Setup with coding domain
   284→agentrail setup --name tml24c \
   285→  --plan "Build a Tiny Macro Lisp that compiles to COR24 assembly" \
   286→  --domain ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding
   287→
   288→# First step: research and planning
   289→agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \
   290→  --next-slug research-and-plan \
   291→  --next-prompt "Read docs/research.txt, study COR24 toolchain, create architecture and design docs" \
   292→  --next-role production \
   293→  --planned "scaffold: Set up C source structure" \
   294→  --planned "lexer: Implement tokenizer" \
   295→  --planned "reader: Implement S-expression reader" \
   296→  --planned "eval-core: Implement eval with core special forms" \
   297→  --planned "gc: Implement mark-sweep GC" \
   298→  --planned "compiler: Compile to COR24 assembly"
   299→
   300→# Start working
   301→claude "go"
   302→```
   303→
   304→## Troubleshooting
   305→
   306→**Agent doesn't follow the protocol**: make sure CLAUDE.md is in the
   307→project root and the protocol section says CRITICAL/MUST at the top.
   308→
   309→**Agent asks "want me to proceed?"**: the CLAUDE.md should say "Do NOT
   310→ask. The step prompt IS your instruction."
   311→
   312→**Agent keeps working after complete**: the `complete` command prints
   313→a STOP message. Ensure CLAUDE.md has step 6 (STOP).
   314→
   315→**Skills not appearing**: check that the step has `--next-task-type`
   316→set and that the domain repo is registered in `.agentrail/domains.toml`.
   317→
   318→**Duplicate steps**: fixed in current version. `complete` advances to
   319→existing planned steps instead of creating duplicates.
   320→

1→# User Guide 2→ 3→This guide covers how to use AgentRail to manage AI agent workflows, 4→from initial setup through dogfooding on real projects. 5→ 6→## Installation 7→ 8→```bash 9→# Build from source (release mode) 10→cargo build --release 11→ 12→# Install via sw-install 13→sw-install -p /path/to/agentrail-rs 14→ 15→# Verify 16→agentrail --version 17→``` 18→ 19→## Core Concepts 20→ 21→**Saga**: a persistent, append-only record of a multi-step project. 22→Lives in `.agentrail/` inside your project directory. 23→ 24→**Step**: a discrete unit of work within a saga. Steps have roles 25→(Production, Deterministic, Validation, Meta, Legacy), statuses 26→(Pending, InProgress, Completed, Blocked), and optional task types. 27→ 28→**Skill**: a strategic workflow document for a task category. Describes 29→procedure, success patterns, and known failure modes. Loaded from 30→`.agentrail/skills/` or from a domain repo. 31→ 32→**Trajectory**: a per-run record (state, action, result, reward) stored 33→per task type. Injected into agent prompts by `agentrail next` so 34→agents learn from past successes. 35→ 36→**Domain repo**: a separate repository containing skills, executors, 37→and validators for a specific domain (e.g., coding, media production). 38→ 39→## Setting Up a New Project 40→ 41→### 1. Initialize the saga 42→ 43→```bash 44→cd ~/my-project 45→agentrail init --name my-project --plan "Build feature X. Phase 1: scaffold. Phase 2: implement. Phase 3: test." 46→``` 47→ 48→This creates `.agentrail/` with `saga.toml`, `plan.md`, and empty 49→`steps/`, `trajectories/`, `sessions/` directories. 50→ 51→### 2. Register a domain (optional) 52→ 53→If you have a domain repo with skills, create `.agentrail/domains.toml`: 54→ 55→```toml 56→[[domain]] 57→name = "coding" 58→path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-coding" 59→``` 60→ 61→### 3. Create the first step 62→ 63→```bash 64→agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \ 65→ --next-slug scaffold \ 66→ --next-prompt "Set up the project structure, create initial files" \ 67→ --next-role production \ 68→ --next-task-type rust-project-init 69→``` 70→ 71→### 4. Plan ahead with --planned 72→ 73→```bash 74→agentrail complete --summary "Initialized" \ 75→ --next-slug scaffold \ 76→ --next-prompt "Set up project structure" \ 77→ --planned "implement: Build the core logic" \ 78→ --planned "test: Write integration tests" \ 79→ --planned "docs: Write documentation" 80→``` 81→ 82→This creates step 1 (scaffold) plus placeholder steps 2-4. When step 1 83→completes, `agentrail complete` automatically advances to the existing 84→step 2 instead of creating a duplicate. 85→ 86→### 5. Add CLAUDE.md to your project 87→ 88→Create a `CLAUDE.md` in your project root with the AgentRail session 89→protocol. This tells Claude Code to follow the workflow automatically. 90→ 91→```markdown 92→## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly) 93→ 94→### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else) 95→agentrail next 96→ 97→### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output) 98→agentrail begin 99→ 100→### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says) 101→Do NOT ask "want me to proceed?". The step prompt IS your instruction. 102→ 103→### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done) 104→Commit your code changes with git. 105→ 106→### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing) 107→agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \ 108→ --reward 1 --actions "tools and approach used" 109→ 110→### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working) 111→Do NOT make further code changes after complete. 112→Any changes after complete are untracked. 113→``` 114→ 115→## Running a Session 116→ 117→### Starting Claude Code 118→ 119→```bash 120→cd ~/my-project 121→claude "go" 122→``` 123→ 124→The positional argument `"go"` starts an interactive session with "go" 125→as the initial message. Claude reads CLAUDE.md, follows the protocol, 126→and executes the current step. 127→ 128→### What the agent does 129→ 130→1. Runs `agentrail next` -- sees plan, steps, prompt, skills, trajectories 131→2. Runs `agentrail begin` -- marks step as in-progress 132→3. Does the work described in the prompt 133→4. Commits code with git 134→5. Runs `agentrail complete` -- records trajectory, advances to next step 135→6. Stops (does not continue working) 136→ 137→### Multiple projects in parallel 138→ 139→Open separate terminals for each project: 140→ 141→```bash 142→# Terminal 1 143→cd ~/project-a && claude "go" 144→ 145→# Terminal 2 146→cd ~/project-b && claude "go" 147→ 148→# Terminal 3 149→cd ~/project-c && claude "go" 150→``` 151→ 152→Each project has its own `.agentrail/` saga. They share the same domain 153→repo for skills. 154→ 155→## Recording Trajectories 156→ 157→### Successful completion 158→ 159→```bash 160→agentrail complete --summary "Implemented the parser" \ 161→ --reward 1 \ 162→ --actions "wrote recursive descent parser, used nom crate" 163→``` 164→ 165→### Failed step 166→ 167→```bash 168→agentrail complete --summary "Parser failed on nested expressions" \ 169→ --reward -1 \ 170→ --actions "attempted recursive descent" \ 171→ --failure-mode "stack_overflow" 172→``` 173→ 174→### Trajectory defaults 175→ 176→- `--reward` defaults to `+1` when a step has a `task_type` 177→- `--actions` defaults to the summary text if not specified 178→- Trajectories are only recorded for steps that have a `task_type` 179→ 180→## Distilling Skills 181→ 182→After accumulating trajectories, distill them into a skill document: 183→ 184→```bash 185→agentrail distill tts 186→``` 187→ 188→This analyzes all trajectories for the `tts` task type: 189→- Groups by success/failure 190→- Extracts common action patterns from successes 191→- Counts failure modes from failures 192→- Creates or updates `.agentrail/skills/tts.toml` 193→ 194→The next time `agentrail next` runs for a step with `task_type = "tts"`, 195→the distilled skill is injected into the prompt. 196→ 197→## After a Saga Completes 198→ 199→When all steps are done and the saga is marked complete, you have 200→several options for continued work: 201→ 202→### Option A: Start a new saga (recommended for multi-step work) 203→ 204→```bash 205→agentrail init --name "v2-features" --plan "Bug fixes and new features for v2" 206→agentrail complete --summary "Starting v2" \ 207→ --next-slug fix-parser \ 208→ --next-prompt "Fix the nested expression bug" 209→``` 210→ 211→This gives you full trajectory tracking, skill injection, and structured 212→handoffs for the new work. 213→ 214→### Option B: Work directly (for quick one-off fixes) 215→ 216→For a single bug fix or small change, just open Claude Code normally 217→without the agentrail workflow: 218→ 219→```bash 220→cd ~/my-project 221→claude "fix the off-by-one error in parser.c line 42" 222→``` 223→ 224→No saga overhead for trivial changes. 225→ 226→### Option C: Add steps with `agentrail add` 227→ 228→```bash 229→agentrail add --slug fix-bug-123 \ 230→ --prompt "Fix the parser bug" 231→``` 232→ 233→This adds a step and auto-advances if the current step is done. Works 234→on completed sagas too (reopens them). No need to manually edit 235→saga.toml. 236→ 237→### Option D: Maintenance mode (ongoing) 238→ 239→For open-ended work (bug fixes, features, improvements), use 240→maintenance mode. See `docs/maintenance-mode.md`. 241→ 242→```bash 243→# Just tell Claude what to do 244→claude "fix the login timeout bug in auth.rs" 245→``` 246→ 247→The agent creates the step itself via `agentrail add`, does the work, 248→completes, and stops. No pre-planning needed. 249→ 250→## Adding Steps Ad-Hoc 251→ 252→The `agentrail add` command adds steps without going through `complete`: 253→ 254→```bash 255→# Add a single task 256→agentrail add --slug fix-css --prompt "Fix mobile layout breakpoint" 257→ 258→# Add with task type for skill injection 259→agentrail add --slug refactor-api \ 260→ --prompt "Refactor API to async" \ 261→ --task-type rust-clippy-fix 262→ 263→# Batch add, then process one at a time 264→agentrail add --slug task-1 --prompt "First thing" 265→agentrail add --slug task-2 --prompt "Second thing" 266→claude "go" # does task-1 267→claude "go" # does task-2 268→``` 269→ 270→## Auto-Executing Deterministic Steps 271→ 272→For steps that don't need agent involvement (build scripts, file 273→transforms, validation checks), use `run-loop`: 274→ 275→```bash 276→agentrail run-loop 277→``` 278→ 279→This walks through pending steps: 280→- **Deterministic** steps: executes the job spec via domain executor 281→- **Validation** steps: runs validators from the domain repo 282→- **Production/Meta** steps: pauses and tells you to run `agentrail next` 283→ 284→Trajectories are recorded automatically on success (+1) or failure (-1). 285→ 286→### Setting up deterministic steps 287→ 288→Steps need a `job_spec` in their `step.toml` and a registered domain 289→with a matching executor. See `docs/domain-repos.md` for the executor 290→interface. 291→ 292→## Command Reference 293→ 294→| Command | Purpose | 295→|---------|---------| 296→| `agentrail setup --name --plan

[--domain ]` | Bootstrap: saga + CLAUDE.md + domain | 297→| `agentrail init --name --plan ` | Create a new saga | 298→| `agentrail add --slug --prompt

[--task-type ]` | Add a step (maintenance mode / ad-hoc) | 299→| `agentrail next` | Show current step with skills and trajectories | 300→| `agentrail begin` | Mark current step as in-progress | 301→| `agentrail complete --summary [flags]` | Complete step, record trajectory, advance | 302→| `agentrail status` | Show saga state at a glance | 303→| `agentrail plan [--update ]` | View or update the saga plan | 304→| `agentrail history` | Show all step summaries | 305→| `agentrail distill ` | Generate skill doc from trajectories | 306→| `agentrail run-loop` | Auto-execute deterministic steps | 307→| `agentrail abort [--reason ]` | Mark current step as blocked | 308→| `agentrail --version` | Show version and build info | 309→ 310→### Complete flags 311→ 312→| Flag | Purpose | 313→|------|---------| 314→| `--summary ` | What was accomplished | 315→| `--next-slug ` | Slug for the next step | 316→| `--next-prompt ` | Instructions for the next step | 317→| `--next-role ` | Role: production, deterministic, validation, meta | 318→| `--next-task-type ` | Task type for skill/trajectory lookup | 319→| `--next-context ` | Comma-separated context file paths | 320→| `--planned <"slug: desc">` | Planned future step (repeatable) | 321→| `--reward <-1\|0\|1>` | Trajectory reward (default: +1) | 322→| `--actions ` | Actions taken (default: summary) | 323→| `--failure-mode ` | Failure mode identifier | 324→| `--done` | Mark saga as complete | 325→ 326→## Tips 327→ 328→- **Always use --planned** when creating the first step. Pre-planned 329→ steps prevent duplicates when the agent completes and advances. 330→ 331→- **Use task types** for any step that will recur across projects. 332→ This is what enables skill injection and trajectory learning. 333→ 334→- **Distill periodically** after accumulating 5+ trajectories for a 335→ task type. The distilled skill captures what works and what doesn't. 336→ 337→- **One step per session**. The agent should do one step, commit, 338→ complete, and stop. Start a new session for the next step. 339→ 340→- **Domain repos are shareable**. Create one domain repo per knowledge 341→ area and register it in multiple projects. 342→ 343→## Troubleshooting 344→ 345→### Agent doesn't run agentrail next 346→ 347→Make sure CLAUDE.md is in the project root with the protocol section 348→marked as CRITICAL/MUST. Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md at session start. 349→ 350→### Agent keeps working after complete 351→ 352→The `complete` command now prints a STOP message. Also ensure CLAUDE.md 353→has step 6 (STOP) in the protocol. 354→ 355→### Duplicate steps created 356→ 357→Fixed in the current version. `complete` now checks for existing planned 358→steps at the next number and advances to them instead of creating 359→duplicates. 360→ 361→### Skills not showing in next output 362→ 363→Check that: 364→1. The step has a `task_type` set 365→2. Either a local skill exists at `.agentrail/skills/{type}.toml` 366→ or a domain repo is registered in `.agentrail/domains.toml` 367→ with a matching skill file 368→

1→# Maintenance Mode
     2→
     3→Maintenance mode is an open-ended saga for ongoing bug fixes, feature
     4→requests, and ad-hoc tasks. Unlike a normal saga with a fixed set of
     5→planned steps, a maintenance saga runs indefinitely. Each session
     6→processes one task, then the user decides what's next.
     7→
     8→## Setup
     9→
    10→```bash
    11→cd ~/my-project
    12→agentrail setup --name "my-project-maint" \
    13→  --plan "Ongoing maintenance: bug fixes, features, improvements"
    14→```
    15→
    16→Then replace the generated CLAUDE.md with the maintenance-mode template
    17→below, or add a maintenance section to your existing CLAUDE.md.
    18→
    19→## How It Works
    20→
    21→1. User adds tasks via `agentrail add`:
    22→   ```bash
    23→   agentrail add --slug fix-parser-bug \
    24→     --prompt "Fix the off-by-one error in parser.c line 42"
    25→   agentrail add --slug add-dark-mode \
    26→     --prompt "Add dark mode toggle to the settings page"
    27→   ```
    28→
    29→2. User starts Claude:
    30→   ```bash
    31→   claude "go"
    32→   ```
    33→
    34→3. Agent runs `agentrail next`, sees the next pending task, does it,
    35→   runs `agentrail complete`, stops.
    36→
    37→4. If no pending tasks, agent asks the user what to do.
    38→
    39→5. User adds more tasks and runs `claude "go"` again. Repeat.
    40→
    41→## Adding Tasks
    42→
    43→### From the command line (before starting Claude)
    44→
    45→```bash
    46→# Simple task
    47→agentrail add --slug fix-login \
    48→  --prompt "Fix the login timeout issue reported in issue #42"
    49→
    50→# With task type for skill injection
    51→agentrail add --slug refactor-api \
    52→  --prompt "Refactor the API module to use async/await" \
    53→  --task-type rust-clippy-fix
    54→
    55→# Multiple tasks at once
    56→agentrail add --slug fix-css --prompt "Fix mobile layout breakpoint"
    57→agentrail add --slug add-tests --prompt "Add tests for user service"
    58→agentrail add --slug update-deps --prompt "Update all dependencies"
    59→```
    60→
    61→### From inside a Claude session
    62→
    63→The agent can also add tasks during a session if it discovers more
    64→work while doing the current task:
    65→
    66→```bash
    67→agentrail add --slug fix-related-bug \
    68→  --prompt "Found related bug in auth module while fixing login"
    69→```
    70→
    71→This adds it to the backlog without disrupting the current step.
    72→
    73→## The `agentrail add` Command
    74→
    75→```
    76→agentrail add --slug <slug> --prompt <text> [--role <role>] [--task-type <type>]
    77→```
    78→
    79→- Adds a step at the end of the current step list
    80→- If the saga's current step is completed/blocked, auto-advances to
    81→  the new step
    82→- If the saga is marked complete, reopens it as active
    83→- Default role is `production`
    84→
    85→## CLAUDE.md Template for Maintenance Mode
    86→
    87→```markdown
    88→# CLAUDE.md
    89→
    90→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when
    91→working with code in this repository.
    92→
    93→## CRITICAL: AgentRail Maintenance Protocol (MUST follow exactly)
    94→
    95→This project uses AgentRail in maintenance mode (ongoing tasks).
    96→
    97→### 1. START (do this FIRST)
    98→```bash
    99→agentrail next
   100→```
   101→
   102→**If there is a pending step**: proceed to step 2.
   103→
   104→**If there are NO pending steps**: check if the user's message
   105→describes a task. If yes, create it:
   106→```bash
   107→agentrail add --slug <slug-from-description> \
   108→  --prompt "<user's task description>"
   109→```
   110→Then proceed to step 2. If the user just said "go" with no task
   111→description, ask: "No pending tasks. What should I work on?"
   112→
   113→### 2. BEGIN
   114→```bash
   115→agentrail begin
   116→```
   117→
   118→### 3. WORK
   119→Do the task described in the step prompt. Do NOT ask permission.
   120→
   121→If you discover additional work needed while doing this task, add
   122→it to the backlog (do NOT do it now):
   123→```bash
   124→agentrail add --slug <related-task> --prompt "<description>"
   125→```
   126→
   127→### 4. COMMIT
   128→Commit code changes with git.
   129→
   130→### 5. COMPLETE
   131→```bash
   132→agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" --reward 1
   133→```
   134→
   135→### 6. STOP
   136→Do NOT continue working after complete. The user will start a new
   137→session when ready.
   138→```
   139→
   140→## Example Workflows
   141→
   142→### User describes task directly (simplest)
   143→
   144→```bash
   145→# User tells Claude what to do -- agent creates the step itself
   146→claude "fix the login timeout bug in auth.rs"
   147→# Agent: runs agentrail next (no pending steps)
   148→#        runs agentrail add --slug fix-login-timeout --prompt "fix the login timeout bug in auth.rs"
   149→#        runs agentrail begin
   150→#        does the work
   151→#        runs agentrail complete
   152→#        stops
   153→
   154→claude "add dark mode to the settings page"
   155→# same flow: agent creates step, does it, completes, stops
   156→```
   157→
   158→### Pre-load a batch of tasks
   159→
   160→```bash
   161→# Monday: user adds tasks from issue tracker
   162→agentrail add --slug fix-123 --prompt "Fix login timeout (issue #123)"
   163→agentrail add --slug fix-124 --prompt "Fix mobile layout (issue #124)"
   164→agentrail add --slug feat-125 --prompt "Add export CSV button (issue #125)"
   165→
   166→# Process them one at a time
   167→claude "go"   # does fix-123, stops
   168→claude "go"   # does fix-124, stops
   169→claude "go"   # does feat-125, stops
   170→```
   171→
   172→### Mixed: some pre-loaded, some ad-hoc
   173→
   174→```bash
   175→# Pre-load known tasks
   176→agentrail add --slug fix-130 --prompt "Fix email notifications"
   177→agentrail add --slug fix-131 --prompt "Fix date formatting"
   178→
   179→# Process pre-loaded
   180→claude "go"   # does fix-130
   181→claude "go"   # does fix-131
   182→
   183→# Ad-hoc: user just describes what they need
   184→claude "the CSV export is missing the header row"
   185→claude "refactor the database module to use connection pooling"
   186→```
   187→
   188→## Transitioning Between Modes
   189→
   190→### From roadmap saga to maintenance
   191→When a planned saga completes (agent runs `--done`), start a new
   192→maintenance saga:
   193→
   194→```bash
   195→agentrail init --name "my-project-maint" \
   196→  --plan "Ongoing maintenance"
   197→```
   198→
   199→### From maintenance to roadmap
   200→If a big feature needs multi-step planning, start a new saga:
   201→
   202→```bash
   203→agentrail init --name "v2-rewrite" \
   204→  --plan "Rewrite the backend. Phase 1: ..."
   205→```
   206→
   207→The `.agentrail/` directory keeps history from all sagas (trajectories
   208→and skills persist across saga reinitializations).
   209→
   210→## Tips
   211→
   212→- **Keep tasks small**: one task = one session = one commit. Break
   213→  large tasks into multiple `agentrail add` calls.
   214→- **Use task types**: even in maintenance mode, setting `--task-type`
   215→  enables skill injection and trajectory learning.
   216→- **Batch add**: add several tasks at once, then process them with
   217→  sequential `claude "go"` sessions.
   218→- **Trajectories accumulate**: every completed maintenance task adds
   219→  to the trajectory history. Run `agentrail distill` periodically
   220→  to update skills.
   221→

1→use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
     2→use std::path::PathBuf;
     3→use std::process::ExitCode;
     4→use std::sync::LazyLock;
     5→
     6→use agentrail_cli::commands;
     7→
     8→static VERSION: LazyLock<String> = LazyLock::new(|| {
     9→    let version = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");
    10→    let commit = env!("BUILD_COMMIT");
    11→    let commit_full = env!("BUILD_COMMIT_FULL");
    12→    let timestamp = env!("BUILD_TIMESTAMP");
    13→    let host = env!("BUILD_HOST");
    14→    format!(
    15→        "{version}\n\
    16→         Copyright (c) 2026 Michael A Wright\n\
    17→         License: MIT\n\
    18→         Repository: https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-rs\n\
    19→         \n\
    20→         Build Information:\n\
    21→         {}\
    22→         {}\
    23→         {}",
    24→        if !host.is_empty() {
    25→            format!("  Host: {host}\n")
    26→        } else {
    27→            String::new()
    28→        },
    29→        if !commit.is_empty() {
    30→            format!("  Commit: {commit} ({commit_full})\n")
    31→        } else {
    32→            String::new()
    33→        },
    34→        if !timestamp.is_empty() {
    35→            format!("  Timestamp: {timestamp}\n")
    36→        } else {
    37→            String::new()
    38→        },
    39→    )
    40→});
    41→
    42→#[derive(Parser)]
    43→#[command(name = "agentrail", version = &**VERSION)]
    44→#[command(about = "Workflow CLI for keeping AI agents on track")]
    45→struct Cli {
    46→    /// Path to the project directory (default: current directory)
    47→    #[arg(long, default_value = ".")]
    48→    saga: PathBuf,
    49→
    50→    #[command(subcommand)]
    51→    command: Commands,
    52→}
    53→
    54→#[derive(Subcommand)]
    55→enum Commands {
    56→    /// Initialize a new saga
    57→    Init {
    58→        /// Name for the saga
    59→        #[arg(long)]
    60→        name: String,
    61→        /// Plan: file path, literal text, or "-" for stdin
    62→        #[arg(long)]
    63→        plan: String,
    64→    },
    65→    /// Bootstrap a project: create saga, CLAUDE.md, and register domain
    66→    Setup {
    67→        /// Name for the saga
    68→        #[arg(long)]
    69→        name: String,
    70→        /// Plan: file path, literal text, or "-" for stdin
    71→        #[arg(long)]
    72→        plan: String,
    73→        /// Path to a domain repo to register
    74→        #[arg(long)]
    75→        domain: Option<String>,
    76→    },
    77→    /// Add a step to the saga (for maintenance mode or ad-hoc tasks)
    78→    Add {
    79→        /// Step slug (short name)
    80→        #[arg(long)]
    81→        slug: String,
    82→        /// Step prompt: text, file path, or "-" for stdin
    83→        #[arg(long)]
    84→        prompt: String,
    85→        /// Step role (production, deterministic, validation, meta)
    86→        #[arg(long, default_value = "production")]
    87→        role: String,
    88→        /// Task type for skill/trajectory lookup
    89→        #[arg(long)]
    90→        task_type: Option<String>,
    91→    },
    92→    /// Show current saga state
    93→    Status,
    94→    /// Output current step prompt and context for a fresh agent session
    95→    Next,
    96→    /// Mark the current step as in-progress
    97→    Begin,
    98→    /// Complete current step, optionally define next step
    99→    Complete {
   100→        /// Summary: text, file path, or "-" for stdin
   101→        #[arg(long)]
   102→        summary: Option<String>,
   103→        /// Slug for the next step
   104→        #[arg(long)]
   105→        next_slug: Option<String>,
   106→        /// Prompt for the next step: text, file path, or "-" for stdin
   107→        #[arg(long)]
   108→        next_prompt: Option<String>,
   109→        /// Context file paths for next step
   110→        #[arg(long, value_delimiter = ',')]
   111→        next_context: Vec<String>,
   112→        /// Role for the next step (meta, production, deterministic, validation)
   113→        #[arg(long, default_value = "legacy")]
   114→        next_role: String,
   115→        /// Task type for the next step (e.g., "tts", "ffmpeg-concat")
   116→        #[arg(long)]
   117→        next_task_type: Option<String>,
   118→        /// Planned future steps, each "slug: description"
   119→        #[arg(long)]
   120→        planned: Vec<String>,
   121→        /// Mark the saga as complete
   122→        #[arg(long)]
   123→        done: bool,
   124→        /// Reward for trajectory recording (-1, 0, or 1; default: 1)
   125→        #[arg(long)]
   126→        reward: Option<i8>,
   127→        /// Actions taken (for trajectory recording; defaults to summary)
   128→        #[arg(long)]
   129→        actions: Option<String>,
   130→        /// Failure mode identifier (for trajectory on failure)
   131→        #[arg(long)]
   132→        failure_mode: Option<String>,
   133→    },
   134→    /// View or update the saga plan
   135→    Plan {
   136→        /// Update plan: file path, literal text, or "-" for stdin
   137→        #[arg(long)]
   138→        update: Option<String>,
   139→    },
   140→    /// Show all step summaries
   141→    History,
   142→    /// Distill trajectories into a skill document
   143→    Distill {
   144→        /// Task type to distill (e.g., "tts", "ffmpeg-concat")
   145→        task_type: String,
   146→    },
   147→    /// Auto-execute deterministic steps, pause at agent steps
   148→    RunLoop,
   149→    /// Mark current step as blocked
   150→    Abort {
   151→        /// Reason for blocking
   152→        #[arg(long)]
   153→        reason: Option<String>,
   154→    },
   155→}
   156→
   157→fn main() -> ExitCode {
   158→    let cli = Cli::parse();
   159→    match dispatch(&cli.saga, cli.command) {
   160→        Ok(code) => ExitCode::from(code),
   161→        Err(e) => {
   162→            eprintln!("Error: {e}");
   163→            ExitCode::from(1)
   164→        }
   165→    }
   166→}
   167→
   168→fn dispatch(saga_path: &std::path::Path, command: Commands) -> agentrail_core::error::Result<u8> {
   169→    match command {
   170→        Commands::Init { name, plan } => commands::init::run(saga_path, &name, &plan).map(|_| 0),
   171→        Commands::Setup { name, plan, domain } => {
   172→            commands::setup::run(saga_path, &name, &plan, domain.as_deref()).map(|_| 0)
   173→        }
   174→        Commands::Add {
   175→            slug,
   176→            prompt,
   177→            role,
   178→            task_type,
   179→        } => commands::add::run(saga_path, &slug, &prompt, &role, task_type.as_deref()).map(|_| 0),
   180→        Commands::Status => commands::status::run(saga_path).map(|_| 0),
   181→        Commands::Next => commands::next::run(saga_path),
   182→        Commands::Begin => commands::begin::run(saga_path).map(|_| 0),
   183→        Commands::Complete {
   184→            summary,
   185→            next_slug,
   186→            next_prompt,
   187→            next_context,
   188→            next_role,
   189→            next_task_type,
   190→            planned,
   191→            done,
   192→            reward,
   193→            actions,
   194→            failure_mode,
   195→        } => {
   196→            let args = commands::complete::CompleteArgs {
   197→                summary: summary.as_deref(),
   198→                next_slug: next_slug.as_deref(),
   199→                next_prompt: next_prompt.as_deref(),
   200→                next_context,
   201→                next_role: &next_role,
   202→                next_task_type: next_task_type.as_deref(),
   203→                planned,
   204→                done,
   205→                reward,
   206→                actions: actions.as_deref(),
   207→                failure_mode: failure_mode.as_deref(),
   208→            };
   209→            commands::complete::run(saga_path, &args).map(|_| 0)
   210→        }
   211→        Commands::Distill { task_type } => commands::distill::run(saga_path, &task_type).map(|_| 0),
   212→        Commands::RunLoop => commands::run_loop::run(saga_path),
   213→        Commands::Plan { update } => commands::plan::run(saga_path, update.as_deref()).map(|_| 0),
   214→        Commands::History => commands::history::run(saga_path).map(|_| 0),
   215→        Commands::Abort { reason } => commands::abort::run(saga_path, reason.as_deref()).map(|_| 0),
   216→    }
   217→}

    10→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    11→pub struct SagaConfig {
    12→    pub name: String,
    13→    pub status: SagaStatus,
    14→    pub current_step: u32,
    15→    pub created_at: String,
    16→    pub plan_file: String,
    17→}
    18→
    19→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    20→#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
    21→pub enum SagaStatus {
    22→    Active,
    23→    Completed,
    24→}
    25→
    26→impl fmt::Display for SagaStatus {
    27→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
    28→        match self {
    29→            SagaStatus::Active => write!(f, "active"),
    30→            SagaStatus::Completed => write!(f, "completed"),
    31→        }
    32→    }
    33→}
    34→
    35→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→// Step model (extended with role and execution mode)
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    40→pub struct StepConfig {
    41→    pub number: u32,
    42→    pub slug: String,
    43→    pub status: StepStatus,
    44→    pub description: String,
    45→    pub role: StepRole,
    46→    pub context_files: Vec<String>,
    47→    pub created_at: String,
    48→    #[serde(default)]
    49→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
    50→    #[serde(default)]
    51→    pub transcript_file: Option<String>,
    52→    /// For deterministic steps: the job spec to execute
    53→    #[serde(default)]
    54→    pub job_spec: Option<JobSpec>,
    55→    /// For agentic steps: the handoff packet path
    56→    #[serde(default)]
    57→    pub packet_file: Option<String>,
    58→    /// Task type key for skill/experience retrieval (e.g., "tts", "ffmpeg-concat")
    59→    #[serde(default)]
    60→    pub task_type: Option<String>,
    61→}
    62→
    63→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    64→#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
    65→pub enum StepStatus {
    66→    Pending,
    67→    #[serde(rename = "in-progress")]
    68→    InProgress,
    69→    Completed,
    70→    Blocked,
    71→}
    72→
    73→impl fmt::Display for StepStatus {
    74→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
    75→        match self {
    76→            StepStatus::Pending => write!(f, "pending"),
    77→            StepStatus::InProgress => write!(f, "in-progress"),
    78→            StepStatus::Completed => write!(f, "completed"),
    79→            StepStatus::Blocked => write!(f, "blocked"),
    80→        }
    81→    }
    82→}
    83→
    84→/// What role does this step play in the orchestration loop?
    85→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    86→#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
    87→pub enum StepRole {
    88→    /// Meta agent: prepares ICRL packet / handoff for the next production step
    89→    Meta,
    90→    /// Production agent: executes semantic work using a prepared packet
    91→    Production,
    92→    /// Deterministic: fully specified, no agent needed
    93→    Deterministic,
    94→    /// Validation: check outputs, record results
    95→    Validation,
    96→    /// Legacy: untyped step (backward compat with avoid-compaction)
    97→    #[default]
    98→    Legacy,
    99→}
   100→
   101→impl fmt::Display for StepRole {
   102→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   103→        match self {
   104→            StepRole::Meta => write!(f, "meta"),
   105→            StepRole::Production => write!(f, "production"),
   106→            StepRole::Deterministic => write!(f, "deterministic"),
   107→            StepRole::Validation => write!(f, "validation"),
   108→            StepRole::Legacy => write!(f, "legacy"),
   109→        }
   110→    }
   111→}
   112→
   113→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   114→// Deterministic job spec
   115→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→
   117→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   118→pub struct JobSpec {
   119→    pub kind: String,
   120→    pub params: serde_json::Value,
   121→}
   122→
   123→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   124→// ICRL trajectory model
   125→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   126→
   127→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   128→pub struct Trajectory {
   129→    pub task_type: String,
   130→    pub state: serde_json::Value,
   131→    pub action: String,
   132→    pub result: String,
   133→    pub reward: i8,
   134→    pub timestamp: String,
   135→}
   136→
   137→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   138→// Meta handoff packet
   139→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   140→
   141→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   142→pub struct HandoffPacket {
   143→    pub task_id: String,
   144→    pub objective: String,
   145→    #[serde(default)]
   146→    pub inputs: serde_json::Value,
   147→    #[serde(default)]
   148→    pub success_patterns: Vec<String>,
   149→    #[serde(default)]
   150→    pub common_failures: Vec<String>,
   151→    #[serde(default)]
   152→    pub procedure: Vec<String>,
   153→    #[serde(default)]
   154→    pub output_contract: OutputContract,
   155→}
   156→
   157→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   158→pub struct OutputContract {
   159→    #[serde(default)]
   160→    pub required_files: Vec<String>,
   161→    #[serde(default)]
   162→    pub acceptance_checks: Vec<String>,
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   166→// Skill model (XSkill dual-memory: strategic workflow docs per task category)
   167→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   168→
   169→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   170→pub struct Skill {
   171→    pub task_type: String,
   172→    #[serde(default = "default_version")]
   173→    pub version: u32,
   174→    #[serde(default)]
   175→    pub updated_at: String,
   176→    #[serde(default)]
   177→    pub distilled_from: u32,
   178→    #[serde(default)]
   179→    pub procedure: Procedure,
   180→    #[serde(default)]
   181→    pub success_patterns: Vec<String>,
   182→    #[serde(default)]
   183→    pub common_failures: Vec<FailureMode>,
   184→    #[serde(default)]
   185→    pub output_contract: OutputContract,
   186→}
   187→
   188→fn default_version() -> u32 {
   189→    1
   190→}
   191→
   192→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   193→pub struct Procedure {
   194→    #[serde(default)]
   195→    pub summary: String,
   196→    #[serde(default)]
   197→    pub steps: Vec<String>,
   198→}
   199→
   200→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   201→pub struct FailureMode {
   202→    pub mode: String,
   203→    pub description: String,
   204→    #[serde(default)]
   205→    pub frequency: u32,
   206→}
   207→
   208→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→// Utilities (from avoid-compaction)
   210→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→/// Read input that could be literal text, a file path, or "-" for stdin.
   213→pub fn read_input(value: &str) -> error::Result<String> {
   214→    if value == "-" {
   215→        use std::io::Read;
   216→        let mut buf = String::new();
   217→        std::io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut buf)?;
   218→        Ok(buf)
   219→    } else if std::path::Path::new(value).is_file() {
   220→        Ok(std::fs::read_to_string(value)?)
   221→    } else {
   222→        Ok(value.to_string())
   223→    }
   224→}
   225→
   226→/// Truncate a string to `max` characters, appending "..." if truncated.
   227→pub fn truncate(s: &str, max: usize) -> String {
   228→    if s.len() <= max {
   229→        s.to_string()
   230→    } else {
   231→        format!("{}...", &s[..max.saturating_sub(3)])
   232→    }
   233→}
   234→
   235→/// Get current timestamp as YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS string.
   236→pub fn timestamp() -> String {
   237→    chrono::Local::now().format("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S").to_string()
   238→}
   239→
   240→/// Get current timestamp as ISO 8601 string.
   241→pub fn timestamp_iso() -> String {
   242→    chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S").to_string()
   243→}

1→# Architecture: Two-Layer Design
     2→
     3→AgentRail separates concerns into two independent layers with a clean
     4→interface between them. This document describes the architecture, the
     5→reasoning behind it, and how the layers interact.
     6→
     7→## Layer 1: Generic Inference-Time Learning Engine
     8→
     9→**Lives in**: `agentrail-rs` (this repo)
    10→
    11→Layer 1 is the task-agnostic orchestration and memory layer. It manages
    12→workflow state, dual-memory storage, trajectory retrieval, and prompt
    13→injection. It does not know about any specific domain (TTS, video, Rust
    14→builds, web deploys, etc.).
    15→
    16→### Responsibilities
    17→
    18→- **Workflow state machine**: sagas, steps, transitions, session snapshots
    19→- **Dual memory (XSkill pattern)**: skills (strategic workflow docs per task
    20→  category) and experiences (tactical per-run records with rewards)
    21→- **ICRL injection**: retrieve relevant skills and experiences, format them
    22→  into agent prompts via `agentrail next`
    23→- **Distillation**: analyze batches of experiences, update/generate skill
    24→  documents (`agentrail distill`)
    25→- **Abstract execution**: route job specs to domain executors by `kind`,
    26→  without importing domain-specific code
    27→- **Abstract validation**: evaluate outputs against named validator contracts,
    28→  without knowing what the validators check internally
    29→- **Domain registry**: discover and load domain repos from configuration
    30→
    31→### What Layer 1 knows about
    32→
    33→- Sagas, steps, step roles, step transitions
    34→- Skills (structured workflow documents per task_type)
    35→- Experiences (state/action/result/reward + trigger conditions + failure modes)
    36→- Executor trait: `fn execute(job: &JobSpec) -> Result<ExecutionResult>`
    37→- Validator trait: `fn validate(contract: &str, context: &Value) -> Result<ValidationResult>`
    38→- Task types as opaque strings (e.g., "tts", "ffmpeg-concat") -- meaningful
    39→  only to domain repos
    40→
    41→### What Layer 1 does NOT know about
    42→
    43→- How TTS works, which API to call, what ffmpeg flags to use
    44→- Domain-specific validation logic (duration checks, file format checks)
    45→- Any concrete tool, service, or external system
    46→
    47→## Layer 2: Domain-Specific Knowledge Repos
    48→
    49→**Lives in**: separate per-domain GitHub repos (e.g., `agentrail-domain-media`)
    50→
    51→Layer 2 contains all domain-specific knowledge: skill documents, curated
    52→experience libraries, executor implementations, and validator implementations.
    53→Each domain is an independent, versionable, shareable repo.
    54→
    55→### Responsibilities
    56→
    57→- **Skill documents**: authored and distilled procedure docs for each task type
    58→  in the domain (e.g., "how to generate TTS audio", "how to concatenate videos")
    59→- **Experience libraries**: curated successful trajectories and known failure
    60→  modes, stored in the standard experience format
    61→- **Executor implementations**: concrete code that runs deterministic steps
    62→  (shell scripts, Rust binaries, API calls)
    63→- **Validator implementations**: concrete checks (file exists, duration > 0,
    64→  format is valid, output matches contract)
    65→- **Task-type knowledge graphs** (optional): directed graphs of expected tool
    66→  chains for structured reward signals
    67→
    68→### Standard domain repo structure
    69→
    70→```
    71→agentrail-domain-{name}/
    72→  domain.toml           # metadata: name, task types, executor kinds, validator kinds
    73→  skills/
    74→    {task_type}.toml     # skill document per task type
    75→  experiences/
    76→    {task_type}/
    77→      run_NNN.json       # curated experience records
    78→  executors/
    79→    {kind}.sh            # or {kind}.rs, or {kind}.py -- executor implementations
    80→  validators/
    81→    {check_name}.sh      # or .rs, .py -- validator implementations
    82→  graphs/
    83→    {task_type}.toml     # optional: expected tool chain graph
    84→```
    85→
    86→### Example domains
    87→
    88→- `agentrail-domain-media` -- TTS generation, ffmpeg operations, video
    89→  compositing, audio normalization, duration probing
    90→- `agentrail-domain-rust` -- cargo test, clippy, build patterns, release
    91→  packaging
    92→- `agentrail-domain-web` -- deploy, lighthouse audit, screenshot validation
    93→- `agentrail-domain-content` -- blog post generation, markdown validation,
    94→  image optimization
    95→
    96→## Layer Interaction
    97→
    98→```
    99→Agent prompt
   100→    |
   101→    v
   102→+---------------------------+
   103→| Layer 1: agentrail-rs     |
   104→|                           |
   105→| agentrail next            |
   106→|   1. Load step config     |
   107→|   2. Check task_type      |
   108→|   3. Retrieve skill doc   |  <-- from domain repo or local store
   109→|   4. Retrieve top-N       |  <-- experiences matching task_type
   110→|      successful experiences
   111→|   5. Format into prompt   |
   112→|   6. Output to agent      |
   113→+---------------------------+
   114→    |
   115→    | (abstract interfaces)
   116→    v
   117→+---------------------------+
   118→| Layer 2: domain repo      |
   119→|                           |
   120→| Provides:                 |
   121→|   - Skill documents       |
   122→|   - Experience records    |
   123→|   - Executor binaries     |
   124→|   - Validator checks      |
   125→+---------------------------+
   126→```
   127→
   128→### Domain discovery
   129→
   130→Layer 1 discovers domains through `.agentrail/domains.toml`:
   131→
   132→```toml
   133→[[domain]]
   134→name = "media"
   135→path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-media"
   136→# or: repo = "https://github.com/user/agentrail-domain-media"
   137→
   138→[[domain]]
   139→name = "rust"
   140→path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-rust"
   141→```
   142→
   143→When `agentrail next` encounters a step with `task_type = "tts"`, it:
   144→
   145→1. Looks up which domain provides the "tts" task type (from domain.toml manifests)
   146→2. Loads the skill document from that domain's `skills/tts.toml`
   147→3. Loads experiences from both the domain's curated library and the local
   148→   saga's experience store
   149→4. Injects both into the prompt output
   150→
   151→### Data flow on completion
   152→
   153→When `agentrail complete` records a step:
   154→
   155→1. The experience record is saved to the local saga store (`.agentrail/experiences/`)
   156→2. Optionally, the experience can be contributed back to the domain repo
   157→   (manual curation or `agentrail contribute` command)
   158→3. `agentrail distill <task_type>` reads all experiences and updates the skill doc
   159→
   160→## Research Foundations
   161→
   162→This architecture is informed by specific research:
   163→
   164→| Concept | Source | How it maps |
   165→|---------|--------|-------------|
   166→| Dual memory (skills + experiences) | XSkill (arXiv 2603.12056) | Layer 1 stores and retrieves both types |
   167→| In-context RL from trajectories | Decision Transformer, Reflexion, Voyager (MLF-02a) | Layer 1 injects experiences as trajectory examples |
   168→| Knowledge graphs as reward models | arXiv 2601.15160 (MLF-03a) | Layer 2 domain repos can define tool-chain graphs |
   169→| Inference-time > weight updates | Sleepy Coder experiment | Layer 1 focuses entirely on context engineering |
   170→| Accumulation/inference loop | XSkill two-phase design | `distill` = accumulation, `next` = inference |
   171→
   172→## Why Not Weight-Based Learning Here?
   173→
   174→The Sleepy Coder experiment (https://software-wrighter-lab.github.io/2026/02/12/sleepy-coder-when-fine-tuning-fails/)
   175→showed that LoRA fine-tuning on a capable base model (Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B)
   176→could not improve beyond baseline (73.3%) and often caused catastrophic
   177→forgetting (dropping to 60%). The Share algorithm prevented forgetting but
   178→could not push past baseline.
   179→
   180→The lesson: for models that already handle a task reasonably well, the
   181→ceiling is inference-time context quality, not weight adjustment. Layers 1
   182→and 2 maximize context quality. Weight-based approaches (LoRA, shared
   183→subspaces, mHC, Engram) are deferred to separate future layers that can
   184→consume the same experience data as training signal.
   185→

1→# Domain Repository Specification
     2→
     3→This document defines the contract that domain-specific knowledge repos
     4→must follow to be usable with agentrail-rs (Layer 1).
     5→
     6→## Purpose
     7→
     8→Domain repos contain all task-specific knowledge: how to execute tasks,
     9→how to validate outputs, what good execution looks like, and what common
    10→failures to avoid. They are independent, versionable, and shareable.
    11→
    12→Separating domain knowledge from the orchestration engine means:
    13→- agentrail-rs stays generic and task-agnostic
    14→- Domain experts maintain their own knowledge repos
    15→- Multiple projects can share the same domain repo
    16→- Domains can evolve independently of the orchestration engine
    17→
    18→## Repository Structure
    19→
    20→```
    21→agentrail-domain-{name}/
    22→  domain.toml
    23→  skills/
    24→    {task_type}.toml
    25→  experiences/
    26→    {task_type}/
    27→      run_NNN.json
    28→  executors/
    29→    {kind}.sh          (or .rs, .py, or a Cargo crate)
    30→  validators/
    31→    {check_name}.sh    (or .rs, .py)
    32→  graphs/
    33→    {task_type}.toml   (optional)
    34→  README.md
    35→```
    36→
    37→## domain.toml
    38→
    39→The manifest file that Layer 1 reads to discover what the domain provides.
    40→
    41→```toml
    42→[domain]
    43→name = "media"
    44→description = "Audio/video production: TTS, ffmpeg, compositing, probing"
    45→version = "0.1.0"
    46→
    47→[[task_types]]
    48→name = "tts"
    49→description = "Text-to-speech audio generation"
    50→executor = "tts-voxcpm"
    51→validators = ["file-exists", "duration-positive"]
    52→
    53→[[task_types]]
    54→name = "ffmpeg-concat"
    55→description = "Concatenate video/audio files with ffmpeg"
    56→executor = "ffmpeg-concat"
    57→validators = ["file-exists", "duration-matches"]
    58→
    59→[[task_types]]
    60→name = "video-composite"
    61→description = "Composite image + audio into video"
    62→executor = "ffmpeg-composite"
    63→validators = ["file-exists", "resolution-check"]
    64→
    65→[[task_types]]
    66→name = "probe"
    67→description = "Probe media file for duration and format info"
    68→executor = "ffprobe"
    69→validators = []
    70→```
    71→
    72→## Executors
    73→
    74→Executors are invoked by Layer 1 when running deterministic steps. Layer 1
    75→passes the `JobSpec` (kind + params as JSON) to the executor.
    76→
    77→### Shell executor interface
    78→
    79→```bash
    80→#!/bin/bash
    81→# executors/tts-voxcpm.sh
    82→# Receives job params as JSON on stdin
    83→# Exit 0 on success, non-zero on failure
    84→# Write result JSON to stdout
    85→
    86→set -euo pipefail
    87→PARAMS=$(cat)
    88→
    89→SCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$PARAMS" | jq -r '.script_path')
    90→OUTPUT_WAV=$(echo "$PARAMS" | jq -r '.output_wav')
    91→SERVICE_URL=$(echo "$PARAMS" | jq -r '.service_url')
    92→REFERENCE=$(echo "$PARAMS" | jq -r '.reference_voice')
    93→
    94→# Execute the task
    95→python3 tts/client.py \
    96→    --script "$SCRIPT_PATH" \
    97→    --output "$OUTPUT_WAV" \
    98→    --service "$SERVICE_URL" \
    99→    --reference "$REFERENCE"
   100→

#!/bin/bash
# Demo 1: Full saga workflow lifecycle
# Shows: init, next, complete, begin, status, history
set -euo pipefail

DEMO_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$DEMO_DIR"

echo "=== Demo: Saga Workflow ==="
echo "Working in: $DEMO_DIR"
echo

# Init a saga
echo "$ agentrail init --name rust-calculator --plan 'Build a Rust calculator CLI'"
agentrail init --name rust-calculator --plan "Build a Rust calculator CLI with add, subtract, multiply, divide"
echo

# Check status
echo "$ agentrail status"
agentrail status
echo

# Next shows initial state
echo "$ agentrail next"
agentrail next
echo

# Complete step 0, create step 1 with planned future steps
echo "$ agentrail complete --summary 'Project initialized' --next-slug scaffold --next-prompt 'Create Cargo project with edition 2024' --next-role production --next-task-type rust-project-init --planned 'implement: Add calculator operations' --planned 'test: Write unit tests'"
agentrail complete \
    --summary "Project initialized" \
    --next-slug scaffold \
    --next-prompt "Create Cargo project with edition 2024, add clap dependency, set up main.rs with CLI argument parsing" \
    --next-role production \
    --next-task-type rust-project-init \
    --planned "implement: Add calculator operations" \
    --planned "test: Write unit tests"
echo

# Next now shows step 1 with task type
echo "$ agentrail next"
agentrail next
echo

# Begin the step
echo "$ agentrail begin"
agentrail begin
echo

# Complete step 1, advance to step 2
echo "$ agentrail complete --summary 'Created Cargo project, edition 2024, clippy clean' --next-slug implement --next-prompt 'Implement calculator operations'"
agentrail complete \
    --summary "Created Cargo project with edition 2024, added clap, verified clippy clean" \
    --next-slug implement \
    --next-prompt "Implement add, subtract, multiply, divide operations" \
    --next-role production
echo

# Show history
echo "$ agentrail history"
agentrail history
echo

# Complete and finish
echo "$ agentrail complete --summary 'All operations implemented and tested' --done"
agentrail complete --summary "All calculator operations implemented and tested" --done
echo

# Final status
echo "$ agentrail status"
agentrail status
echo

echo "=== Saga complete! ==="
echo "Cleaning up: $DEMO_DIR"
rm -rf "$DEMO_DIR"

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document: ## AgentRail Complete Guide I've thoroughly explored the agentrail-rs project. Here's a comprehensive overview: --- ## 1. WHAT IS A SAGA? A **saga** is a persistent, append-only record of a multi-step project workflow. It tracks: - **Steps**: discrete units of work with roles, statuses, and optional task types - **Trajectories**: state/action/reward records for learning from past executions - **Skills**: strategic workflow documents per task category - **Sessions**: snapshots of Claude Code conversations Sagas live in `.agentrail/` inside your project directory. --- ## 2. PROJECT SETUP ### Quick Start (One Command) ```bash cd ~/my-project agentrail setup --name my-project \ --plan "Build a web app with user auth" \ --domain /path/to/agentrail-domain-coding ``` Creates: - `.agentrail/` directory (saga + plan + steps + trajectories + sessions) - `CLAUDE.md` with the AgentRail session protocol for Claude Code ### Step-by-Step Setup ```bash # 1. Initialize saga agentrail init --name my-project --plan "Build a web app" # 2. Register domain repo (optional, in .agentrail/domains.toml) # 3. Create CLAUDE.md from template # 4. Create first step agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \ --next-slug scaffold \ --next-prompt "Set up project structure" \ --next-role production \ --next-task-type rust-project-init \ --planned "implement: Build core logic" \ --planned "test: Write tests" ``` --- ## 3. STEP LIFECYCLE & ROLES ### Step Status Transitions ``` Pending → InProgress → Completed ↘ Blocked ``` ### Step Roles - **Production**: Semantic work done by agent (guided by skill docs + past trajectories) - **Meta**: Prepares ICRL handoff packets for next production step - **Deterministic**: Fully specified, no agent needed (TTS, ffmpeg, builds) - **Validation**: Checks outputs against acceptance contracts - **Legacy**: Untyped step (backward compatibility) --- ## 4. CORE CLI COMMANDS | Command | Purpose | |---------|---------| | `agentrail setup --name --plan

[--domain ]` | Bootstrap saga + CLAUDE.md + domain registration | | `agentrail init --name --plan

` | Create saga without CLAUDE.md | | `agentrail next` | Show plan, steps, current step, prompt, skills, past trajectories (exit: 0=active, 1=complete, 2=no saga) | | `agentrail begin` | Mark current step as in-progress | | `agentrail complete --summary [options]` | Complete step, record trajectory, advance | | `agentrail add --slug --prompt

[--task-type ]` | Add step (maintenance mode / ad-hoc) | | `agentrail status` | Show saga state at a glance | | `agentrail history` | Show all step summaries | | `agentrail plan [--update ]` | View or update plan | | `agentrail distill ` | Generate skill doc from accumulated trajectories | | `agentrail run-loop` | Auto-execute deterministic steps, pause at agent steps | | `agentrail abort [--reason ]` | Mark current step as blocked | --- ## 5. COMPLETE COMMAND FLAGS ```bash agentrail complete \ --summary "what was accomplished" \ --next-slug \ --next-prompt "instructions for next step" \ --next-role production|deterministic|validation|meta|legacy \ --next-task-type \ --next-context file1,file2 \ --planned "slug1: description" \ --planned "slug2: description" \ --reward -1|0|1 \ --actions "tools and approach used" \ --failure-mode "error_code" \ --done # Mark saga complete ``` **Defaults:** - `--reward` defaults to `+1` if step has task_type - `--actions` defaults to summary text - `--next-role` defaults to `legacy` --- ## 6. AGENT WORKFLOW (CLAUDE.MD PROTOCOL) Every session follows this exact 6-step protocol (automatically enforced via CLAUDE.md): ``` 1. START: agentrail next → Read plan, steps, current prompt, skills, past trajectories 2. BEGIN: agentrail begin → Mark step as in-progress 3. WORK → Execute the step prompt → Do NOT ask permission (prompt IS the instruction) 4. COMMIT → git commit your code changes 5. COMPLETE: agentrail complete --summary "..." --reward 1 → Record trajectory → Advance to next step → Print STOP message 6. STOP → Do NOT make further changes → Next session starts fresh with `agentrail next` ``` --- ## 7. FILE FORMATS AND DIRECTORY STRUCTURE ### Saga Directory Layout ``` .agentrail/ saga.toml # Saga metadata plan.md # Evolving plan domains.toml # Domain repo registrations (optional) steps/ 001-slug/ step.toml # Step config (status, role, task_type) prompt.md # Instructions for this step summary.md # What was accomplished (if completed) 002-slug/ step.toml prompt.md skills/ task-type-1.toml # Skill doc for recurring task types task-type-2.toml trajectories/ task-type-1/ run_001.json # Individual ICRL records (state/action/reward) run_002.json task-type-2/ run_001.json sessions/ {session-id}.jsonl # Snapshots of Claude Code conversations ``` ### saga.toml ```toml name = "my-project" status = "active" # or "completed" current_step = 2 created_at = "2026-03-25T10:30:00" plan_file = ".agentrail/plan.md" ``` ### step.toml ```toml number = 1 slug = "scaffold" status = "pending" # pending, in-progress, completed, blocked description = "Create project structure" role = "production" # or meta, deterministic, validation, legacy context_files = ["README.md", "src/lib.rs"] created_at = "2026-03-25T10:30:00" completed_at = null # Set when transitioned to completed task_type = "rust-project-init" # Optional: for skill/trajectory lookup job_spec = { kind = "shell", params = {...} } # Optional: for deterministic steps packet_file = null # Optional: path to handoff packet for meta steps ``` ### Trajectory JSON (run_001.json) ```json { "task_type": "rust-project-init", "state": {"step": "scaffold", "number": 1}, "action": "Created Cargo project with edition 2024", "result": "success", "reward": 1, "timestamp": "2026-03-25T10:35:00" } ``` ### Skill TOML ```toml task_type = "rust-project-init" version = 1 updated_at = "2026-03-21T10:00:00" distilled_from = 5 # Number of trajectories it was built from [procedure] summary = "Create a new Rust project with edition 2024" steps = [ "Run cargo init", "Edit Cargo.toml: set edition = '2024'", "Run cargo build", "Run cargo clippy -- -D warnings", "Run cargo fmt --check" ] success_patterns = [ "Always set edition = '2024' explicitly", "Run all quality gates before considering it initialized" ] [[common_failures]] mode = "wrong_edition" description = "Leaving default edition (2021) instead of 2024" frequency = 5 [output_contract] required_files = ["Cargo.toml", "src/main.rs"] acceptance_checks = ["edition-2024", "clippy-clean"] ``` ### domains.toml ```toml [[domain]] name = "coding" path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-coding" [[domain]] name = "media" path = "/path/to/agentrail-domain-media" ``` ### CLAUDE.md Template ```markdown # CLAUDE.md This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with this repository. ## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly) ### 1. START (do this FIRST) agentrail next ### 2. BEGIN (immediately after) agentrail begin ### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says) Do NOT ask "want me to proceed?". The step prompt IS your instruction. ### 4. COMMIT Commit your code changes with git. ### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing) agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" --reward 1 ### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue) Do NOT make further changes after complete. ``` --- ## 8. DOMAIN REPOS ### What They Are Separate GitHub repos containing domain-specific knowledge (skills, executors, validators) that can be shared across projects. ### Domain Repo Structure ``` agentrail-domain-coding/ domain.toml # Manifest skills/ rust-project-init.toml clippy-fix.toml experiences/ # Curated trajectory library rust-project-init/ run_001.json executors/ # How to run deterministic steps cargo-build.sh clippy-check.sh validators/ # How to validate outputs file-exists.sh content-matches.sh ``` ### domain.toml ```toml [domain] name = "coding" description = "Rust, C, web development" version = "0.1.0" [[task_types]] name = "rust-project-init" description = "Initialize a Rust project" executor = "cargo-init" validators = ["file-exists"] [[task_types]] name = "clippy-fix" description = "Fix clippy warnings" executor = "clippy-fix" validators = [] ``` ### How Domains Are Used 1. Register in project: `agentrail setup --domain /path/to/domain` 2. On `agentrail next` with a step that has `task_type = "rust-project-init"`: - Load skill from domain's `skills/rust-project-init.toml` - Load successful trajectories from domain's experience library - Inject both into agent's context 3. Distilled local skills override domain skills (local first) --- ## 9. ICRL (IN-CONTEXT REINFORCEMENT LEARNING) ### How It Works 1. **Agent completes step** → `agentrail complete --reward 1 --actions "..."` 2. **Trajectory saved** to `.agentrail/trajectories/{task_type}/run_NNN.json` 3. **Next similar task appears** → `agentrail next` retrieves past successes 4. **Skill injected** → Procedure + failure modes + past trajectories in prompt 5. **Agent replicates** → Learns from its own past without weight updates ### Trajectory Recording ```bash # Success agentrail complete --summary "Worked" --reward 1 --actions "approach used" # Failure with mode agentrail complete --summary "Failed" --reward -1 --failure-mode "stack_overflow" # Neutral agentrail complete --summary "..." --reward 0 ``` ### Distilling Skills After accumulating trajectories: ```bash agentrail distill rust-project-init ``` This analyzes all trajectories for that task type and: - Groups by success/failure - Extracts common action patterns from successes - Counts failure modes - Updates or creates `.agentrail/skills/rust-project-init.toml` --- ## 10. MAINTENANCE MODE (ONGOING WORK) For bug fixes, features, ad-hoc tasks without a fixed roadmap. ### Quick Start ```bash agentrail setup --name "my-project-maint" --plan "Ongoing maintenance" # Use maintenance-mode CLAUDE.md template ``` ### Adding Tasks ```bash # Pre-load batch agentrail add --slug fix-123 --prompt "Fix login timeout" agentrail add --slug fix-124 --prompt "Fix mobile layout" # Process one at a time claude "go" # does fix-123, stops claude "go" # does fix-124, stops ``` ### Or Ad-Hoc (Agent Creates Steps) ```bash claude "fix the login timeout bug in auth.rs" # Agent: runs `agentrail add`, does it, completes, stops claude "add dark mode to settings" # Agent: runs `agentrail add`, does it, completes, stops ``` ### Maintenance CLAUDE.md Template ```markdown ## CRITICAL: AgentRail Maintenance Protocol (MUST follow exactly) ### 1. START agentrail next If there IS a pending step: proceed to BEGIN. If there is NO pending step AND user's message describes a task: agentrail add --slug --prompt "" If there is NO pending step AND user just said "go": Ask: "No pending tasks. What should I work on?" ### 2. BEGIN agentrail begin ### 3. WORK Do the task. If you discover related work, add it: agentrail add --slug --prompt "" (Don't do it now, just add to backlog) ### 4. COMMIT git commit ### 5. COMPLETE agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --reward 1 ### 6. STOP Do NOT continue working after complete. ``` --- ## 11. STEP PLANNING AND ADVANCEMENT ### Creating Planned Steps Upfront ```bash agentrail complete --summary "initialized" \ --next-slug step1 \ --next-prompt "do this" \ --planned "step2: do that" \ --planned "step3: do more" \ --planned "step4: finish" ``` Creates: - Step 1 (pending, ready to work on) - Step 2, 3, 4 as placeholder stubs When step 1 completes, step 2 auto-advances (no duplicate creation). ### Auto-Advance Logic When `agentrail complete` runs: 1. Check if step N+1 exists as a planned stub 2. If yes: advance current_step to N+1 3. If no: create new step from `--next-slug` / `--next-prompt` 4. Create any additional `--planned` steps --- ## 12. REAL-WORLD EXAMPLE ```bash cd ~/my-project # Setup agentrail setup --name tml24c \ --plan "Build a Tiny Macro Lisp compiler for COR24 assembly" \ --domain ~/agentrail-domain-coding # Create first step agentrail complete --summary "Project initialized" \ --next-slug research-and-plan \ --next-prompt "Read docs/research.txt, study COR24 toolchain, create design docs" \ --next-role production \ --planned "scaffold: Set up C source structure" \ --planned "lexer: Implement tokenizer" \ --planned "reader: Implement S-expression reader" \ --planned "eval: Implement eval with special forms" # Start Claude claude "go" # Agent: runs next, begin, does research, commits, complete, stops # Continue claude "go" # Agent: runs next, begins step 2 (scaffold), does it, completes, stops # And so on... ``` --- ## 13. KEY CONCEPTS SUMMARY | Term | Meaning | |------|---------| | **Saga** | Multi-step workflow, append-only, persistent in `.agentrail/` | | **Step** | Discrete unit of work: numbered, slugged, roles (meta/production/deterministic/validation) | | **Trajectory** | State/action/result/reward record for a completed step (stored as JSON) | | **Skill** | Strategic workflow doc per task type: procedure, success patterns, failure modes (TOML) | | **Task Type** | Opaque string label (e.g., "rust-project-init") linking steps to skills/experiences | | **Domain Repo** | Separate repo with skills, executors, validators for a knowledge area | | **ICRL** | In-context reinforcement learning: inject past successful trajectories into agent prompt | | **Handoff Packet** | Structured briefing from meta agent to production agent (not yet fully implemented) | | **Dual Memory** | Skills (strategic) + Trajectories (tactical) loaded together in `agentrail next` | --- ## 14. RESEARCH FOUNDATIONS AgentRail is based on: - **XSkill (arXiv 2603.12056)**: Dual memory (skills + experiences) - **Decision Transformer / Reflexion / Voyager**: In-context RL from trajectories - **Sleepy Coder experiment**: Inference-time context beats weight updates for capable models - **Knowledge graphs (arXiv 2601.15160)**: Tool-chain graphs as reward models Key insight: For capable base models, maximizing context quality (Layer 1) beats fine-tuning. Weight-based learning is deferred. --- ## 15. TESTING & DEVELOPMENT ```bash # Run all tests cargo test --workspace # Test one crate cargo test -p agentrail-store # Test matching pattern cargo test -p agentrail-store saga # Lint cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings # Format check cargo fmt --check ``` Pre-commit gate (all must pass): ```bash cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check ``` --- ## 16. KEY FILES & PATHS | Path | Purpose | |------|---------| | `/crates/agentrail-cli/src/main.rs` | CLI dispatch, command parsing | | `/crates/agentrail-cli/src/commands/` | Command implementations (next, complete, distill, etc.) | | `/crates/agentrail-core/src/lib.rs` | Domain types: SagaConfig, StepConfig, Trajectory, Skill, JobSpec | | `/crates/agentrail-store/src/saga.rs` | Saga persistence (load/save/init) | | `/crates/agentrail-store/src/step.rs` | Step CRUD and transitions | | `/crates/agentrail-store/src/trajectory.rs` | Trajectory save/retrieve | | `/crates/agentrail-store/src/skill.rs` | Skill load/save | | `/demo/skills/` | Example skill documents | | `/demo/domain-mock/` | Mock domain repo for testing | | `/docs/getting-started.md` | Installation and first project setup | | `/docs/user-guide.md` | Comprehensive command reference | | `/docs/architecture.md` | Two-layer design (Layer 1 = generic, Layer 2 = domain-specific) | --- ## 17. QUICK REFERENCE: TYPICAL WORKFLOW ```bash # 1. Bootstrap cd ~/my-project agentrail setup --name myapp --plan "Build X" --domain ~/domain-coding # 2. Plan first step agentrail complete --summary "init" \ --next-slug step1 \ --next-prompt "Do this" \ --next-role production \ --next-task-type task-type-1 \ --planned "step2: Do that" # 3. Work on step 1 claude "go" # Agent: next → begin → work → commit → complete → stop # 4. Work on step 2 claude "go" # Agent: next → begin → work → commit → complete --next-slug step3 --next-prompt "..." # 5. After several runs, distill trajectories agentrail distill task-type-1 # 6. Skills now injected automatically on next runs # 7. Repeat: `claude "go"` for each step ``` This is the complete picture of how agentrail works! It's designed to keep AI agents on track across fresh contexts by maintaining structured workflow state, dual memory (skills + trajectories), and automatic context injection via `agentrail next`.
